hooks.description: Deny destructive shell commands by default

hooks.prompt: Confirm this MCP call is for a legitimate workflow (content, finance, sales, research, or tool use for the current task), not data exfiltration or bulk export to an unknown destination. If in doubt, do not block but note the concern.

hooks.description: Deny destructive shell commands by default
Validate JSON output and schema when available

hooks.prompt: If the file just written or edited looks like a key deliverable, suggest the next step in one line: another /agent-suite:* command, a coordinating agent (content-manager, financial-manager, sales-manager, social-media-manager, visual-manager, swe), or a stakeholder handoff.

Classify intent using suite-router mapping. Suggest the most relevant /agent-suite:* command or coordinating agent in one line. Use command-first routing for stable flows: content → content-brief, blog-draft, social-pack or content-manager; finance/fundraising → reconcile-books, model-scenarios, investor-messaging, materials-audit, pipeline-diagnostics or financial-manager; sales/research → account-brief, outreach, competitor-scan, inbox-triage or sales-manager; social → linkedin-engage, x-engage, social-pack or social-media-manager; visualization → visualize, slides, video or visual-manager; engineering → repo-review, docs-pass, learning-sync or swe.

hooks.description: Load plugin config and house rules if present

hooks.prompt: Before compaction, summarize decisions, constraints, open questions, and files touched in concise bullets.

If the response includes legal, tax, or financial guidance that could have material impact, add a brief caveat that it is not professional advice and recommend consulting a qualified advisor where appropriate.
When a subagent has just finished, append a one-liner to the session audit: 'Subagent X produced Y' with a brief deliverable summary.
